failure_modes.text: Teams can lock onto one answer too early and never discover better options that only appear once the search space is widened.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when the team is stuck too early on one frame or one answer.
failure_modes.mitigation: Enforce a minimum idea count before any evaluation begins; early convergence kills the search space that produces non-obvious options.

failure_modes.text: Idea generation can become fake divergence when evaluation leaks in and only confirms the group’s current direction.
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate divergent and convergent phases strictly: no critique, ranking, or feasibility filtering during idea generation.

failure_modes.text: The first idea can anchor the session so strongly that later options only orbit around it instead of widening the possibility space.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require each participant to propose at least one idea that contradicts the first proposal; explicit counter-anchoring breaks the orbit.

premortem_questions.text: Are we still genuinely widening the option set, or have we already started evaluating and defending favorites?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Best when divergence is explicitly separated from evaluation.

premortem_questions.text: What assumption is making the first idea feel like the natural reference point for everything else?
What hidden alternative would matter if we forced ourselves to go beyond the current obvious set?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Works when idea volume is genuinely valuable because the search space is still underexplored.

heuristics.text: Separate divergence from evaluation so idea generation stays open long enough to produce real alternatives.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when divergence is explicitly separated from evaluation.

heuristics.text: Push for volume only when the search space is genuinely underexplored and hidden alternatives still matter.
heuristics.source_quote: Works when idea volume is genuinely valuable because the search space is still underexplored.

heuristics.text: Reset the session if the first answer has already become the implicit reference point.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on genuine divergence, anti-anchoring discipline, and separating option generation from evaluation.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor workshop-sprawl because the raw source already supports compact divergence chunks.
open_questions: The source supports broader group-process work, but the Wave 2 surface stays on widening the option set before judgment.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on divergence-friendly exploration models.
antagonists: The source is strongest against confirmation-bias and anchoring during generation.
structured_tensions: The model contains explicit tensions against confirmation-bias and anchoring.
